message.content:
You are mapping the CONTENT HIERARCHY subsystem of an educational platform (Node/TS + MongoDB/Mongoose) at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/Subject.model.ts
- server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts
- server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts
- server/src/routes/subject.routes.ts
Skim subject.json at repo root as an example import.
Goal: document the content hierarchy. Specifically answer:
- Exact schema of Subject and any nested entities (Book, Chapter, Topic, Paragraph). Are books/chapters/topics/paragraphs EMBEDDED subdocuments or SEPARATE collections? What are the id fields, order fields, content fields?
- Does a Subject support MULTIPLE books today? How is ordering of books/chapters/topics/paragraphs handled?
- Is there any notion of "subtopic" (подтема) as a distinct entity, or only topic/paragraph?
- How is content validated on import? Any uniqueness constraints?
Return the structured map. Use exact field names and file:line. Quote the Mongoose schema fields verbatim in evidence.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read the four core files and skim the example import.

content.content:
1	import mongoose, { Schema, Document, Model } from 'mongoose';
2	import {
3	  ISubject,
4	  IBook,
5	  IChapter,
6	  ITopic,
7	  IParagraph,
8	  IContent,
9	  IMetadata,
10	  Difficulty
11	} from '../types';
12	
13	/**
14	 * SUBJECT MODEL
15	 * Корневая модель для иерархической структуры образовательного контента
16	 * Содержит: Subject -> Book -> Chapter -> Topic -> Paragraph -> Content
17	 * 
18	 * Оптимизация: Использую вложенные схемы (embedded documents) вместо ссылок,
19	 * так как контент всегда читается целиком для генерации тестов.
20	 * Это уменьшает количество запросов к БД.
21	 */
22	
23	// ==================== SUB-SCHEMAS ====================
24	
25	const MetadataSchema = new Schema<IMetadata>({
26	  keywords: [{ type: String }],
27	  difficulty: { 
28	    type: String, 
29	    enum: Object.values(Difficulty),
30	    required: false 
31	  },
32	  source: { type: String }
33	}, { _id: false });
34	
35	const ContentSchema = new Schema<IContent>({
36	  text: { 
37	    type: String, 
38	    required: true,
39	    trim: true,
40	    minlength: 1
41	  },
42	  pages: [{ 
43	    type: Number, 
44	    required: true 
45	  }],
46	  metadata: { 
47	    type: MetadataSchema, 
48	    required: true 
49	  }
50	}, { _id: false });
51	
52	const ParagraphSchema = new Schema<IParagraph>({
53	  order: { 
54	    type: Number, 
55	    required: true,
56	    min: 0
57	  },
58	  content: { 
59	    type: ContentSchema, 
60	    required: true 
61	  }
62	}, { timestamps: false });
63	
64	const TopicSchema = new Schema<ITopic>({
65	  title: {
66	    type: String,
67	    required: true,
68	    trim: true,
69	    minlength: 1,
70	    maxlength: 200
71	  },
72	  /** Порядок темы в главе (drag-and-drop). Необязателен для старых данных. */
73	  order: {
74	    type: Number,
75	    min: 0
76	  },
77	  /** Темы КТП (KtpCatalog.topics._id), на которые замаплена эта тема книги. M:N. */
78	  ktpTopicIds: [{ type: Schema.Types.ObjectId }],
79	  paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]
80	}, { timestamps: false });
81	
82	const ChapterSchema = new Schema<IChapter>({
83	  title: { 
84	    type: String, 
85	    required: true,
86	    trim: true,
87	    minlength: 1,
88	    maxlength: 200
89	  },
90	  order: { 
91	    type: Number, 
92	    required: true,
93	    min: 0
94	  },
95	  topics: [TopicSchema]
96	}, { timestamps: false });
97	
98	const BookSchema = new Schema<IBook>({
99	  title: { 
100	    type: String, 
101	    required: true,
102	    trim: true,
103	    minlength: 1,
104	    maxlength: 300
105	  },
106	  author: { 
107	    type: String,
108	    trim: true,
109	    maxlength: 200
110	  },
111	  contentLanguage: {
112	    type: String,
113	    trim: true,
114	    maxlength: 80
115	  },
116	  chapters: [ChapterSchema]
117	}, { timestamps: false });
118	
119	// ==================== MAIN SCHEMA ====================
120	
121	type ISubjectDocument = Document &
122	  Omit<ISubject, '_id'> & {
123	    getBookContent(bookId: string): string;
124	    getChapterContent(bookId: string, chapterId: string): string;
125	    getTopicsContent(bookId: string, chapterId: string, topicIds: string[]): string;
126	  };
127	
128	const SubjectSchema = new Schema<ISubjectDocument>({
129	  title: { 
130	    type: String, 
131	    required: true,
132	    trim: true,
133	    unique: true,
134	    minlength: 1,
135	    maxlength: 200,
136	    index: true
137	  },
138	  description: { 
139	    type: String,
140	    trim: true,
141	    maxlength: 1000
142	  },
143	  /** Основные предметы (общие) vs профильные (для выбора пары ЕНТ) */
144	  subjectKind: {
145	    type: String,
146	    enum: ['main', 'profile'],
147	    default: 'main',
148	    index: true
149	  },
150	  books: [BookSchema]
151	}, { 
152	  timestamps: true,
153	  collection: 'subjects'
154	});
155	
156	// ==================== INDEXES ====================
157	// Индексы для быстрого поиска по вложенным структурам
158	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books._id': 1 });
159	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books.chapters._id': 1 });
160	SubjectSchema.index({ 'books.chapters.topics._id': 1 });
161	
162	// ==================== METHODS ====================
163	
164	/**
165	 * Метод для получения текста всей книги
166	 * Используется для генерации тестов по всей книге
167	 */
168	SubjectSchema.methods.getBookContent = function(bookId: string): string {
169	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
170	  if (!book) return '';
171	  
172	  return book.chapters
173	    .flatMap((chapter: IChapter) => chapter.topics)
174	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
175	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
176	    .join('\n\n');
177	};
178	
179	/**
180	 * Метод для получения текста конкретной главы
181	 * Используется для генерации тестов по главе
182	 */
183	SubjectSchema.methods.getChapterContent = function(bookId: string, chapterId: string): string {
184	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
185	  if (!book) return '';
186	  
187	  const chapter = book.chapters.id(chapterId);
188	  if (!chapter) return '';
189	  
190	  return chapter.topics
191	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
192	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
193	    .join('\n\n');
194	};
195	
196	/**
197	 * Метод для получения текста по выбранным темам
198	 * Используется для генерации тестов по конкретным темам
199	 */
200	SubjectSchema.methods.getTopicsContent = function(bookId: string, chapterId: string, topicIds: string[]): string {
201	  const book = this.books.id(bookId);
202	  if (!book) return '';
203	  
204	  const chapter = book.chapters.id(chapterId);
205	  if (!chapter) return '';
206	  
207	  return chapter.topics
208	    .filter((topic: ITopic) => topicIds.includes(topic._id!.toString()))
209	    .flatMap((topic: ITopic) => topic.paragraphs)
210	    .map((paragraph: IParagraph) => paragraph.content.text)
211	    .join('\n\n');
212	};
213	
214	// ==================== MODEL ====================
215	
216	export const Subject: Model<ISubjectDocument> = mongoose.model<ISubjectDocument>('Subject', SubjectSchema);
217

1	import { Router } from 'express';
2	import { body, param, query } from 'express-validator';
3	import { subjectController } from '../controllers';
4	import { authenticate, isAdmin, isTeacherOrAdmin, asyncHandler, validate } from '../middlewares';
5	
6	/**
7	 * SUBJECT ROUTES
8	 * Маршруты для управления образовательным контентом
9	 */
10	
11	const router = Router();
12	
13	/**
14	 * Публичные маршруты (для гостевого режима)
15	 */
16	router.get(
17	  '/',
18	  [
19	    query('subjectKind')
20	      .optional()
21	      .isIn(['main', 'profile'])
22	      .withMessage('subjectKind must be main or profile')
23	  ],
24	  validate,
25	  asyncHandler(subjectController.getAllSubjects.bind(subjectController))
26	);
27	
28	router.get(
29	  '/:id',
30	  [
31	    param('id')
32	      .isMongoId()
33	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID')
34	  ],
35	  validate,
36	  asyncHandler(subjectController.getSubjectById.bind(subjectController))
37	);
38	
39	// Защищённые маршруты
40	router.use(authenticate);
41	
42	/**
43	 * @route   POST /subjects/import
44	 * @desc    Импорт предмета целиком (с книгами, главами, темами, параграфами)
45	 * @access  Admin only
46	 */
47	router.post(
48	  '/import',
49	  isAdmin,
50	  [
51	    body('title')
52	      .trim()
53	      .isLength({ min: 1, max: 200 })
54	      .withMessage('Title is required'),
55	    body('updateIfExists').optional().isBoolean().withMessage('updateIfExists must be boolean')
56	  ],
57	  validate,
58	  asyncHandler(subjectController.importSubject.bind(subjectController))
59	);
60	
61	/**
62	 * @route   POST /subjects
63	 * @desc    Создать новый предмет
64	 * @access  Admin only
65	 */
66	router.post(
67	  '/',
68	  isAdmin,
69	  [
70	    body('title')
71	      .trim()
72	      .isLength({ min: 1, max: 200 })
73	      .withMessage('Title must be between 1 and 200 characters'),
74	    body('description')
75	      .optional()
76	      .trim()
77	      .isLength({ max: 1000 })
78	      .withMessage('Description must be less than 1000 characters'),
79	    body('subjectKind')
80	      .optional()
81	      .isIn(['main', 'profile'])
82	      .withMessage('subjectKind must be main or profile')
83	  ],
84	  validate,
85	  asyncHandler(subjectController.createSubject.bind(subjectController))
86	);
87	
88	/**
89	 * @route   POST /subjects/:id/books
90	 * @desc    Добавить книгу к предмету
91	 * @access  Admin only
92	 */
93	router.post(
94	  '/:id/books',
95	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
96	  [
97	    param('id')
98	      .isMongoId()
99	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID'),
100	    body('title')
101	      .trim()
102	      .isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 })
103	      .withMessage('Title must be between 1 and 300 characters'),
104	    body('author')
105	      .optional()
106	      .trim()
107	      .isLength({ max: 200 })
108	      .withMessage('Author must be less than 200 characters'),
109	    body('contentLanguage')
110	      .optional()
111	      .trim()
112	      .isLength({ max: 80 })
113	      .withMessage('contentLanguage must be at most 80 characters')
114	  ],
115	  validate,
116	  asyncHandler(subjectController.addBook.bind(subjectController))
117	);
118	
119	/**
120	 * @route   POST /subjects/:subjectId/books/import
121	 * @desc    Импорт одной книги целиком (главы/темы/параграфы), напр. из Excel
122	 * @access  teacher/admin
123	 */
124	router.post(
125	  '/:subjectId/books/import',
126	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
127	  [
128	    param('subjectId').isMongoId(),
129	    body('book').isObject().withMessage('book is required'),
130	    body('book.title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('book.title must be 1..300 chars'),
131	    body('book.chapters').optional().isArray().withMessage('book.chapters must be an array'),
132	    body('book.chapters.*.title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('chapter title must be 1..300 chars'),
133	    body('book.chapters.*.topics').isArray().withMessage('chapter.topics must be an array'),
134	    body('book.chapters.*.topics.*.title').trim().isLength({ min: 1, max: 300 }).withMessage('topic title must be 1..300 chars'),
135	    body('book.chapters.*.topics.*.paragraphs').isArray().withMessage('topic.paragraphs must be an array'),
136	    body('book.chapters.*.topics.*.paragraphs.*.content.text').trim().isLength({ min: 1 }).withMessage('paragraph content.text is required')
137	  ],
138	  validate,
139	  asyncHandler(subjectController.importBook.bind(subjectController))
140	);
141	
142	/**
143	 * @route   POST /subjects/books/:bookId/chapters
144	 * @desc    Добавить главу к книге
145	 * @access  Admin only
146	 */
147	router.post(
148	  '/books/:bookId/chapters',
149	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
150	  [
151	    param('bookId')
152	      .isMongoId()
153	      .withMessage('Invalid book ID'),
154	    query('subjectId')
155	      .isMongoId()
156	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID in query'),
157	    body('title')
158	      .trim()
159	      .isLength({ min: 1, max: 200 })
160	      .withMessage('Title must be between 1 and 200 characters'),
161	    body('order')
162	      .isInt({ min: 0 })
163	      .withMessage('Order must be a non-negative integer')
164	  ],
165	  validate,
166	  asyncHandler(subjectController.addChapter.bind(subjectController))
167	);
168	
169	/**
170	 * @route   POST /subjects/chapters/:chapterId/topics
171	 * @desc    Добавить тему к главе
172	 * @access  Admin only
173	 */
174	router.post(
175	  '/chapters/:chapterId/topics',
176	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
177	  [
178	    param('chapterId')
179	      .isMongoId()
180	      .withMessage('Invalid chapter ID'),
181	    query('subjectId')
182	      .isMongoId()
183	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID in query'),
184	    query('bookId')
185	      .isMongoId()
186	      .withMessage('Invalid book ID in query'),
187	    body('title')
188	      .trim()
189	      .isLength({ min: 1, max: 200 })
190	      .withMessage('Title must be between 1 and 200 characters')
191	  ],
192	  validate,
193	  asyncHandler(subjectController.addTopic.bind(subjectController))
194	);
195	
196	/**
197	 * @route   POST /subjects/topics/:topicId/paragraphs
198	 * @desc    Добавить параграф к теме
199	 * @access  Admin only
200	 */
201	router.post(
202	  '/topics/:topicId/paragraphs',
203	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
204	  [
205	    param('topicId')
206	      .isMongoId()
207	      .withMessage('Invalid topic ID'),
208	    query('subjectId')
209	      .isMongoId()
210	      .withMessage('Invalid subject ID in query'),
211	    query('bookId')
212	      .isMongoId()
213	      .withMessage('Invalid book ID in query'),
214	    query('chapterId')
215	      .isMongoId()
216	      .withMessage('Invalid chapter ID in query'),
217	    body('order')
218	      .isInt({ min: 0 })
219	      .withMessage('Order must be a non-negative integer'),
220	    body('content.text')
221	      .trim()
222	      .isLength({ min: 1 })
223	      .withMessage('Content text is required'),
224	    body('content.pages')
225	      .isArray()
226	      .withMessage('Content pages must be an array'),
227	    body('content.metadata')
228	      .isObject()
229	      .withMessage('Content metadata is required')
230	  ],
231	  validate,
232	  asyncHandler(subjectController.addParagraph.bind(subjectController))
233	);
234	
235	// ========== UPDATE ==========
236	
237	router.patch(
238	  '/:id',
239	  isAdmin,
240	  [
241	    param('id').isMongoId(),
242	    body('subjectKind').optional().isIn(['main', 'profile']).withMessage('subjectKind must be main or profile')
243	  ],
244	  validate,
245	  asyncHandler(subjectController.updateSubject.bind(subjectController))
246	);
247	
248	router.patch(
249	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId',
250	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
251	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId()],
252	  validate,
253	  asyncHandler(subjectController.updateBook.bind(subjectController))
254	);
255	
256	router.patch(
257	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId',
258	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
259	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId()],
260	  validate,
261	  asyncHandler(subjectController.updateChapter.bind(subjectController))
262	);
263	
264	router.patch(
265	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId',
266	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
267	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId(), param('topicId').isMongoId()],
268	  validate,
269	  asyncHandler(subjectController.updateTopic.bind(subjectController))
270	);
271	
272	router.patch(
273	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId/paragraphs/:paragraphId',
274	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
275	  [
276	    param('subjectId').isMongoId(),
277	    param('bookId').isMongoId(),
278	    param('chapterId').isMongoId(),
279	    param('topicId').isMongoId(),
280	    param('paragraphId').isMongoId(),
281	    body('order').optional().isInt({ min: 0 }).withMessage('order must be a non-negative integer'),
282	    body('content').optional().isObject(),
283	    body('content.text').optional().isString().isLength({ min: 1 }).withMessage('content.text must be non-empty'),
284	    body('content.pages').optional().isArray(),
285	    body('content.metadata').optional().isObject()
286	  ],
287	  validate,
288	  asyncHandler(subjectController.updateParagraph.bind(subjectController))
289	);
290	
291	// ========== REORDER (drag-and-drop) ==========
292	
293	router.post(
294	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/reorder',
295	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
296	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), body('orderedChapterIds').isArray(), body('orderedChapterIds.*').isMongoId()],
297	  validate,
298	  asyncHandler(subjectController.reorderChapters.bind(subjectController))
299	);
300	
301	router.post(
302	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/reorder',
303	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
304	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId(), body('orderedTopicIds').isArray(), body('orderedTopicIds.*').isMongoId()],
305	  validate,
306	  asyncHandler(subjectController.reorderTopics.bind(subjectController))
307	);
308	
309	router.post(
310	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId/paragraphs/reorder',
311	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
312	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId(), param('topicId').isMongoId(), body('orderedParagraphIds').isArray(), body('orderedParagraphIds.*').isMongoId()],
313	  validate,
314	  asyncHandler(subjectController.reorderParagraphs.bind(subjectController))
315	);
316	
317	/**
318	 * @route   PUT /subjects/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId/ktp
319	 * @desc    Маппинг темы книги на темы КТП (M:N)
320	 * @access  Admin (в Фазе 5 — teacher+admin)
321	 */
322	router.put(
323	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId/ktp',
324	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
325	  [
326	    param('subjectId').isMongoId(),
327	    param('bookId').isMongoId(),
328	    param('chapterId').isMongoId(),
329	    param('topicId').isMongoId(),
330	    body('ktpTopicIds').optional().isArray().withMessage('ktpTopicIds must be an array'),
331	    body('ktpTopicIds.*').optional().isMongoId().withMessage('ktpTopicIds must contain valid ids')
332	  ],
333	  validate,
334	  asyncHandler(subjectController.setTopicKtp.bind(subjectController))
335	);
336	
337	// ========== DELETE ==========
338	
339	router.delete(
340	  '/:id',
341	  isAdmin,
342	  [param('id').isMongoId()],
343	  validate,
344	  asyncHandler(subjectController.deleteSubject.bind(subjectController))
345	);
346	
347	router.delete(
348	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId',
349	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
350	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId()],
351	  validate,
352	  asyncHandler(subjectController.deleteBook.bind(subjectController))
353	);
354	
355	router.delete(
356	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId',
357	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
358	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId()],
359	  validate,
360	  asyncHandler(subjectController.deleteChapter.bind(subjectController))
361	);
362	
363	router.delete(
364	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId',
365	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
366	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId(), param('topicId').isMongoId()],
367	  validate,
368	  asyncHandler(subjectController.deleteTopic.bind(subjectController))
369	);
370	
371	router.delete(
372	  '/:subjectId/books/:bookId/chapters/:chapterId/topics/:topicId/paragraphs/:paragraphId',
373	  isTeacherOrAdmin,
374	  [param('subjectId').isMongoId(), param('bookId').isMongoId(), param('chapterId').isMongoId(), param('topicId').isMongoId(), param('paragraphId').isMongoId()],
375	  validate,
376	  asyncHandler(subjectController.deleteParagraph.bind(subjectController))
377	);
378	
379	export default router;
380

input.description: Find type definition files and grep for content hierarchy interfaces

content.content:
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts:396:      '- По умолчанию ОДИН урок. Если материала очень много — раздели на 2–4 последовательных урока по подтемам.',

content.text: I have all the information needed. The only "подтем" reference is in a roadmap AI prompt (unrelated to a distinct entity). No subtopic entity exists. Let me compile the structured map.

input.subsystem: Content Hierarchy (Subject → Book → Chapter → Topic → Paragraph → Content)

entities.name: Subject (root collection)
fields: title: String — required, trim, unique, minlength 1, maxlength 200, index
relationships: EMBEDS books as subdocument array (books: [BookSchema], line 150)

Referenced BY other collections via subjectId (Test, CanonicalRoadmap, KtpCatalog, NodeLessonContent, UserRoadmapProgress, RoadmapAttempt, RoadmapChatAttachment, ProfileSubjectPair, User.testHistory) — see deleteSubject cascade subject.controller.ts:288-315
Topic.ktpTopicIds reference KtpCatalog.topics._id (cross-collection M:N, NOT a nested ref)
entities.notes: Stored in collection 'subjects' (line 153). title is the ONLY uniqueness constraint (unique:true line 133). Three extra indexes on embedded ids: 'books._id', 'books.chapters._id', 'books.chapters.topics._id' (lines 158-160). Instance methods getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent concatenate paragraph text (lines 168-212).

entities.name: Book (embedded subdocument)
fields: _id: ObjectId (auto — BookSchema does NOT set _id:false, so each book gets its own _id)
title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 300
contentLanguage: String — optional, trim, maxlength 80 (language for AI generation)
entities.notes: NO 'order' field on Book — book ordering is implicit by array position only. timestamps:false. _id present (used everywhere via b._id?.toString()).

entities.name: Chapter (embedded subdocument)
fields: title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 200
entities.notes: order is required (line 90-94), unlike Topic.order. Server assigns order on add (addChapter: order = book.chapters.length, controller:201) and reorder sets order=index. timestamps:false.

entities.name: Topic (embedded subdocument)
fields: title: String — required, trim, minlength 1, maxlength 200
order: Number — OPTIONAL, min 0 (optional for legacy data; line 72-76)
ktpTopicIds: [ObjectId] — array of KtpCatalog.topics._id, M:N mapping to КТП (line 77-78)
relationships: ktpTopicIds: loose ObjectId refs to KtpCatalog.topics._id (validated against KtpCatalog in setTopicKtp, controller:467-474)
entities.notes: This is the deepest titled entity below chapter. There is NO subtopic/подтема entity. ktpTopicIds is a bare [{type: ObjectId}] with no ref/populate. order optional, set on add (controller:218) and reorder.

entities.name: Paragraph (embedded subdocument)
entities.notes: Holds the actual text payload via content. order required. timestamps:false.

entities.name: Content (embedded, _id:false)
fields: text: String — required, trim, minlength 1 (the actual study text)
pages: [Number] — each required (page numbers in source book)
entities.notes: _id:false (line 50) — no own id. text is what feeds AI test/roadmap generation.

entities.name: Metadata (embedded, _id:false)
fields: difficulty: String — enum Difficulty ['easy','medium','hard'], NOT required

flows.name: Import whole subject (POST /subjects/import)
steps: Route validates only title (1..200) and updateIfExists boolean — subject.routes.ts:47-59. NO deep body validation of books/chapters/topics/paragraphs here.
Controller importSubject reads {title,description,books} from body — subject.controller.ts:58-59
If existing subject with same title and !updateIfExists → 400 error (controller:64-68)
If existing && updateIfExists → Subject.updateOne $set description/subjectKind/books, wholesale REPLACING books array (controller:69-81)
Else Subject.create({title, description, subjectKind, books: books||[]}) — controller:114-119. Mongoose schema validation runs on create (required text, enums, lengths).
Computes stats (book/chapter/topic/paragraph counts) by reducing nested arrays (controller:121-137)
flows.aiCalls: None. Pure DB write. AI consumes this content later via resolveBookContentForAI.

flows.name: Import single book (POST /subjects/:subjectId/books/import)
steps: Route DOES deep-validate the book shape with express-validator wildcards: book.title, book.chapters[*].title, .topics (isArray), .topics[*].title, .topics[*].paragraphs (isArray), .paragraphs[*].content.text required — subject.routes.ts:124-140
Controller importBook pushes one book onto subject.books without touching others, then subject.save() — controller:172-188 (Mongoose validation runs on save)

flows.name: Granular add (addBook/addChapter/addTopic/addParagraph)
steps: addBook: subject.books.push, save — controller:158-169 (book has no order)
addChapter: finds book, push {title, order: book.chapters.length, topics:[]} — server assigns order to end to avoid collisions (controller:191-204)
addTopic: finds book→chapter, push {title, order: chapter.topics.length, paragraphs:[]} — controller:207-221
addParagraph: finds book→chapter→topic, push {order, content} — controller:224-242. subjectId/bookId/chapterId passed via QUERY params, not path.

flows.name: Reorder (drag-and-drop)
steps: reorderChapters/reorderTopics/reorderParagraphs receive ordered id arrays — controller:398-449
assertPermutation guards that posted ids are an exact permutation of existing ids (no missing/dup/foreign) — controller:30-36
Sets each element's order = its index, then save — chapters/topics/paragraphs all use the numeric 'order' field for sort

flows.name: Read content for AI (resolveBookContentForAI)
steps: Subject.findById(dto.subjectId) — subjectContent.service.ts:15
Find book by _id in subject.books (in-memory .find) — service:18
If topicFocus given (no chapterId, not fullBook) → fuzzy-match a chapter by title substring — service:26-34
fullBook or no chapter → subject.getBookContent(bookId) joins all paragraph.content.text with '\n\n' — service:37, model:168-177
Else getChapterContent(bookId, chapterId) joins that chapter's paragraph texts — service:43, model:183-194
Builds IContentForAI {text, metadata{subjectTitle,bookTitle,chapterTitle,topics,bookAuthor,contentLanguage,chapterTitles}} — service:56-68. chapterTitles sorted by chapter.order (service:52-54).
flows.aiCalls: This is the READ path that produces the text payload fed to downstream LLM test/roadmap generation (callers, not in these files). No LLM call here itself; getTopicsContent (model:200-212) supports topic-subset reads.

input.reuse: Content is authored once (import or granular add) and stored as embedded subdocuments inside the single Subject document; it is re-read in full for every AI generation via getBookContent/getChapterContent/getTopicsContent (concatenated text). There is NO caching layer in these files — the AI-ready text (IContentForAI.text) is rebuilt from scratch on every resolveBookContentForAI call by re-joining all paragraph texts. The deliberate design choice (model comment lines 17-20) is embedding-over-references so the whole content tree loads in one Subject.findById query rather than caching. The only persisted derived/reused artifact is Topic.ktpTopicIds (the book-topic→КТП-topic M:N mapping), which is computed by curators and stored, not regenerated.
input.aiCost: No LLM calls occur in this subsystem. It is the content store/read layer that FEEDS AI generation elsewhere. The relevant cost driver it controls is granularity: resolveBookContentForAI returns either the full book text (dto.fullBook or no chapter → getBookContent, every paragraph in the book) or a single chapter's text (getChapterContent). Whole-book selection sends the entire embedded corpus to the LLM, so cost scales with chosen scope (book vs chapter vs topic-subset). Output is per-request and not shared/cached across users — each generation re-concatenates and re-sends the same source text.
problems: MULTIPLE books per Subject ARE supported structurally (books: [BookSchema], unbounded array) and the granular/import APIs append books, but Book has NO 'order' field — book ordering is purely implicit array insertion order, inconsistent with Chapter (required order), Topic (optional order) and Paragraph (required order). subject.json example also has only one book.

Inconsistent ordering model: Chapter.order required (model:90), Paragraph.order required (model:53), Topic.order OPTIONAL (model:73, legacy data), Book has no order at all. Sorting relies on order where present; getBookContent (model:172-176) iterates chapters/topics/paragraphs in stored ARRAY order, NOT by .order, so a stale array order vs order field can desync AI text sequence. Only chapterTitles metadata is explicitly sorted by order (service:52-54).

No uniqueness anywhere except Subject.title (model:133). Books, chapters, topics, paragraphs can be exact duplicates (same title/order) with no constraint — and addChapter/addTopic assign order=array length, so deleting from the middle then adding can produce duplicate order values until a reorder runs.

updateIfExists import REPLACES the entire books array wholesale via $set (controller:72-81). Since all chapter/topic/paragraph _ids are auto-regenerated on re-import, this orphans every external reference keyed on those embedded ids — notably Topic.ktpTopicIds mappings, KtpCatalog links, roadmap/NodeLessonContent that may target old ids. No id preservation on re-import.

Validation asymmetry between the two import paths: /subjects/import does NOT deep-validate book/chapter/topic/paragraph shape at the route (subject.routes.ts:47-59) — relies solely on Mongoose schema validation on create; whereas /subjects/:subjectId/books/import DOES (routes:124-140). content.metadata is schema-required (model:46-49) but subject.json paragraphs omit fields and one even omits difficulty — fine since difficulty optional, but a metadata object is mandatory.

Unbounded document growth: entire subject (all books/chapters/topics/paragraphs/full text) is one MongoDB document. With many books or large texts this risks the 16MB BSON document limit and forces loading the whole tree on every Subject.findById/getAllSubjects (getAllSubjects even selects 'books', controller:145-147, returning full content trees for listing).

ktpTopicIds is a bare [{type: Schema.Types.ObjectId}] with no ref and no schema-level validation that ids exist; validity is enforced only imperatively in setTopicKtp against KtpCatalog (controller:467-474). Direct import of a book with ktpTopicIds would bypass that check.

getBookContent/getChapterContent return '' on missing book/chapter (model:170,184-188) and resolveBookContentForAI then throws badRequest('No content available') (service:48-50) — a missing-vs-empty distinction is lost, so a wrong bookId can surface as a generic content error in some paths.
evidence: BookSchema (model:98-117) has NO order field: `const BookSchema = new Schema<IBook>({ title: {...maxlength:300}, author: {...}, contentLanguage: {...maxlength:80}, chapters: [ChapterSchema] }, { timestamps: false });`
ChapterSchema order REQUIRED (model:90-94): `order: { type: Number, required: true, min: 0 }`

TopicSchema order OPTIONAL + ktpTopicIds (model:72-79): `/** Порядок темы в главе (drag-and-drop). Необязателен для старых данных. */ order: { type: Number, min: 0 }, /** Темы КТП ... M:N. */ ktpTopicIds: [{ type: Schema.Types.ObjectId }], paragraphs: [ParagraphSchema]`
Embedding is deliberate (model:17-20): `Оптимизация: Использую вложенные схемы (embedded documents) вместо ссылок, так как контент всегда читается целиком для генерации тестов.`

Subject embeds books, only title unique (model:128-154): `title: { type: String, required: true, trim: true, unique: true, minlength: 1, maxlength: 200, index: true }` ... `books: [BookSchema]` ... `{ timestamps: true, collection: 'subjects' }`

ParagraphSchema (model:52-62): `order: { type: Number, required: true, min: 0 }, content: { type: ContentSchema, required: true }` — and ContentSchema (model:35-50) `text: { required: true, trim: true, minlength: 1 }, pages: [{ type: Number, required: true }], metadata: { type: MetadataSchema, required: true }`, both with `{ _id: false }`.

getBookContent iterates by array order, not .order (model:172-176): `return book.chapters.flatMap((chapter)=>chapter.topics).flatMap((topic)=>topic.paragraphs).map((paragraph)=>paragraph.content.text).join('\n\n');`
updateIfExists wholesale replace (controller:72-81): `await Subject.updateOne({ _id: existing._id }, { $set: { description: ..., subjectKind, books: booksArr } });`
Server-assigned order on add (controller:201): `book.chapters.push({ title, order: book.chapters.length, topics: [] });`

No subtopic entity anywhere: grep for 'subtopic|подтем|Subtopic|subTopic' across server/src returns only one hit in roadmap.ai.service.ts:396 (an LLM prompt instruction 'раздели на 2–4 ... урока по подтемам'), not a data model.
subject.json example has exactly ONE book object under `books` with chapters[].order = 1,2,3 and topics WITHOUT an order field (topics rely on array position).

content.text:
I have completed the analysis of the content hierarchy subsystem and returned the structured map.

Key findings:

**Hierarchy & storage**: Subject → Book → Chapter → Topic → Paragraph → Content/Metadata. Everything below Subject is an EMBEDDED Mongoose subdocument array, not separate collections (deliberate per the model comment at `Subject.model.ts:17-20`). Only `Subject` is its own collection (`subjects`).

**ids/order/content**:
- Book, Chapter, Topic, Paragraph each get an auto `_id` (Book and Paragraph schemas do NOT set `_id:false`); Content and Metadata are `_id:false`.
- Chapter.order and Paragraph.order are required; Topic.order is OPTIONAL (legacy); **Book has NO order field** — books are ordered only by array position.
- Actual study text lives in `Paragraph.content.text` (`Content` also has `pages` and `metadata{keywords,difficulty,source}`).

**Multiple books**: Structurally yes — `books: [BookSchema]` is unbounded and both import paths and `addBook` append books — but there is no per-book ordering, and `subject.json` ships only one book.

**Subtopic**: No. There is no подтема/subtopic entity. The only `подтем` hit is an LLM prompt string in `roadmap.ai.service.ts:396`, unrelated to the data model. Topic and Paragraph are the deepest titled/content levels.

**Validation & uniqueness**: The only uniqueness constraint is `Subject.title` (`unique:true`). No uniqueness on books/chapters/topics/paragraphs. `/subjects/import` validates only `title` at the route and leans on Mongoose schema validation; `/subjects/:subjectId/books/import` deep-validates the nested book shape with express-validator wildcards (`subject.routes.ts:124-140`). Reorders are guarded by `assertPermutation` (`subject.controller.ts:30-36`).

Relevant files (all absolute):
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/Subject.model.ts`
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/services/subjectContent.service.ts`
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/controllers/subject.controller.ts`
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/routes/subject.routes.ts`
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types/index.ts` (interfaces `IMetadata`/`IContent`/`IParagraph`/`ITopic`/`IChapter`/`IBook`/`ISubject`, lines 20-76)
- `/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/subject.json` (example import)
